name: Leaderboard Telecast
rationale: Renders the contest as a glossy esports / sports-broadcast spectacle — jumbotron Elo bars, slow-mo replay frames, lower-third commentator chyrons, sponsor-logo absurdity — so the joke lands in the visual grammar itself: we treat 'state of the art' with the breathless overproduction of a championship final, then let the held-out scroll deflate it on live air. The agon of the Iliad's funeral games, broadcast.
preamble: High-gloss esports broadcast / sports-telecast graphic style; super-saturated stadium spectacle with a colossal glowing jumbotron leaderboard and animated Elo-rune bar charts; thick lower-third chyron caption bars, score bugs, and 'LIVE' tickers framing the action; dramatic slow-motion replay framing with motion-blur streaks and freeze-frame impact flashes; gladiator-meets-MMA-walkout staging, spotlight cones cutting through arena haze, confetti and pyro; over-the-top sponsor-banner absurdity ringing the sand; mood: a championship final taken far too seriously, then punctured; references: ESPN graphics packages, League of Legends Worlds stage, WWE entrance lighting, fighting-game versus screens; composition splits hype-spectacle on one side against a single small calm judge holding one unglamorous fresh scroll on the other.
